source.description: User-controlled URL string read from query parameter 'url'. No scheme, host, or format check is applied.

propagation.description: The url value passes only an empty-string check and is forwarded unchanged to the HTTP client.

sink.description: requests.get() initiates an outbound HTTP request to the attacker-controlled URL. Internal addresses such as http://169.254.169.254/ are reachable.

expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The URL parameter flows directly into requests.get() with no validation. An attacker can target cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), internal services, or arbitrary internet hosts.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Validate the parsed URL: enforce an https-only scheme allowlist, restrict hostnames to a known domain allowlist, and block private/loopback/link-local IP ranges. Set allow_redirects=False.

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink where the unsanitized URL enters requests.get().
